payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Tracking pocket money earnings strategically
payload.justification: Tracking is described as a fundamental process the enterprise performs to achieve its purpose.

payload.description: Pocket money as a strategic asset tracked by the enterprise
payload.justification: Pocket money represents a thing of strategic importance that the enterprise manages and tracks.

payload.description: Task completion as a strategic element of the enterprise
payload.justification: Task completion is identified as a strategic element through which pocket money is earned, representing a thing of strategic importance.

payload.description: Child user tracking earned pocket money
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies children as strategic stakeholders who track pocket money, representing a primary actor in the enterprise context.

payload.description: Parent user fulfilling payment obligations
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies parents as strategic stakeholders responsible for payment fulfillment, representing a primary actor in the enterprise context.

payload.description: Enable tracking of pocket money earned through task completion
payload.justification: Signal explicitly declares this as the fundamental purpose of the system, representing a strategic goal of the enterprise.

payload.description: Enable parental accountability for payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly expresses this as an underlying goal and system purpose, representing a strategic driver of the enterprise.

payload.description: Pocket money tracking business process through task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG918 defines the system's purpose as tracking pocket money through task completion, representing the core business process.

payload.description: Parent defines tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG919 describes a specific business capability for parents to define tasks.

payload.description: Child views available and completed tasks within weekly timeframe
payload.justification: Signal SG921 describes a business capability for children to view tasks, a discrete function.

payload.description: Child views aggregated weekly earnings
payload.justification: Signal SG922 describes a business capability providing visibility into earnings.

payload.description: Parent views child earnings and task completion status
payload.justification: Signal SG923 describes a business reporting capability as a distinct function.

payload.description: Tasks reset to available state on weekly cycle boundaries
payload.justification: Signal SG924 defines a business rule governing weekly lifecycle and state reset behavior.

payload.description: Task as a business entity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG919 explicitly describes tasks as entities with monetary values, a core business data object.

payload.description: Child as a business entity participating in the pocket money system
payload.justification: Signal SG920 establishes child as an entity in relationship with completed tasks.

payload.description: Weekly earnings as a derived aggregate attribute of child entity
payload.justification: Signal SG922 describes aggregated weekly earnings as a derived concept, representing an attribute calculation.

payload.description: Completion relationship linking child entities to task entities
payload.justification: Signal SG920 explicitly describes a conceptual relationship between completed tasks and children.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle governing task lifecycle and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Signals SG921, SG922, and SG924 all reference weekly timeframes, establishing a weekly business cycle.

payload.description: Weekly reset event initiating task state transition to available
payload.justification: Signal SG924 describes weekly reset behavior, representing a temporal trigger event.

payload.description: Parent role responsible for defining tasks and viewing reports
payload.justification: Signals SG919 and SG923 describe parent responsibilities, establishing parent as a business role.

payload.description: Child role responsible for completing tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: Signals SG920, SG921, and SG922 describe child responsibilities, establishing child as a business role.

payload.description: Enable pocket money tracking through task-based earnings
payload.justification: Signal SG918 describes the system's core purpose, representing the primary business goal.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained for temporal analysis
payload.justification: Signal SG925 describes a business requirement for data retention, representing a business constraint.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Contribution Recognition
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for recognizing and valuing task completion contributions as strategic elements that trigger entitlements, establishing the foundational exchange relationship within the enterprise model.

payload.name: Family Participation Governance
payload.description: Enterprise-level governance defining who holds membership and participates in the household economic framework, establishing the foundational roster of accountable parties and their relational standing within the family enterprise structure.

payload.name: Payment Obligation Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for fulfilling reciprocal financial obligations incurred through recognized contributions, establishing the commitment framework for converting earned entitlements into discharged payment responsibilities.

payload.name: Economic Exchange Rationale
payload.description: Enterprise-level justification for the reciprocal value exchange model that governs household economic relationships, establishing why contribution-based entitlement and obligation structures serve strategic household objectives.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the structure of compensable tasks, including their monetary values and lifecycle states within recurring cycles.

payload.name: Participation Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing child participation in the pocket money system, including their engagement with tasks, completion tracking, and accountability for earned compensation.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for determining and representing the monetary obligations arising from task completion, including aggregation across timeframes and settlement accountability.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise task completion as a strategic element.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to recognise task completion as a foundational entity of strategic importance, derived from the What-column entity CCI.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to track pocket money earned through task completion.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to provide the capability for children to track their earnings from completed tasks, derived from the Why-column goal CCI. The enterprise enables the tracking function without prescribing implementation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants entitled to earn pocket money.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-Who-001 establishes children as a foundational actor class within the family participation governance domain; the enterprise commitment at Row 1 is to recognise this class of participant and their entitlement to participate in the earnings framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants accountable for fulfilling payment obligations.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-Who-002 establishes parents as a foundational actor class within the family participation governance domain; the enterprise commitment at Row 1 is to recognise this class of participant and their accountability for payments.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain accountability for pocket money obligations.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs express the enterprise's commitment to govern and track pocket money as a recognized financial obligation. The What-column CCI identifies pocket money as an enterprise-managed asset; the How-column CCI describes the tracking mechanism. Together they constitute a single governance constraint: the enterprise's accountability for this financial obligation. This is a Why-level commitment (accountability, governance) mapped through What and How evidence.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-Why-001 establishes the enterprise commitment to provide a mechanism by which children can earn pocket money contingent upon completing tasks. This is a functional capability the enterprise commits to enable.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable tracking of pocket money earned by children.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-Why-001 explicitly calls for tracking of earned pocket money. This is a distinct functional obligation from the earning mechanism itself.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to be accountable for fulfilling payment obligations.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-Why-002 establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability that supports parental accountability for payment fulfillment. This is an enablement of accountability rather than a constraint on behavior.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate derived from task completions for each child.
payload.rationale: Realizes tracking capability by establishing business responsibility to maintain the earnings artifact that represents pocket money earned through task completion.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise Child as a business entity participating in the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: Elaborates enterprise recognition of children as participants by defining the Child entity at Row 2 business level.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the Child role as entitled to earn compensation through task completion.
payload.rationale: Establishes the business rule governing child entitlement, elaborating the enterprise commitment to recognize children as entitled participants.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the Parent role as accountable for defining tasks and viewing reports.
payload.rationale: Elaborates enterprise recognition of parents as accountable participants by defining the Parent role and its accountabilities at Row 2.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of the completion relationship linking each child to their completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Realizes accountability for pocket money obligations by maintaining the business artifact that establishes entitlement through task completion.

payload.statement: The business shall retain historical task completion data for temporal analysis.
payload.rationale: Elaborates accountability obligation by imposing a retention constraint that ensures auditability of payment obligations over time.

payload.statement: The business shall govern task lifecycle through a weekly business cycle.
payload.rationale: Realizes earning enablement by establishing the business responsibility to maintain the temporal framework within which earning opportunities recur.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly cycle boundary triggers task state reset.

payload.statement: The business shall reset tasks to available state on weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Elaborates earning enablement by establishing the business rule that renews earning opportunities, ensuring continuous task availability.
payload.fit_criteria: All tasks transition to available state at weekly reset event.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings aggregates visible to children.
payload.rationale: Realizes tracking enablement by providing the system affordance that presents earnings information to the entitled child actor.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings visible to parents.
payload.rationale: Realizes tracking enablement by providing the system affordance that presents earnings information to the parent actor.

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion status visible to parents.
payload.rationale: Realizes tracking enablement by providing the system affordance that presents task completion information to the parent actor.

payload.statement: The parent shall be accountable for fulfilling payment obligations arising from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Realizes enterprise enablement of parent accountability by establishing the business rule that assigns fulfillment responsibility to the parent role.

payload.statement: The Task conceptual entity shall define: attributes: monetary_value money (new), availability_status lifecycle_state (new).

attributes.name: monetary_value
attributes.description: The monetary compensation associated with completing the task

attributes.name: availability_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the task within the weekly cycle

payload.rationale: Task is the core business entity for compensable work, carrying monetary value and lifecycle state as essential conceptual attributes at the business-owner level.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly reset cycle for task availability state transitions.
payload.rationale: This is a business governance rule constraining when task state transitions occur; the weekly cycle boundary is a temporal constraint on business behavior, requiring the business to enforce the reset timing.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task records for temporal analysis.
payload.rationale: This is a business responsibility (behind the boundary) to maintain an artefact (historical records); retention vocabulary is avoided per Row 2 guidance in favor of 'maintain a record'.

payload.statement: The Child conceptual entity shall define: attributes: participant_identifier identifier (new), participant_name name (new), participation_status lifecycle_state (new).

attributes.name: participant_identifier
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the child participant

attributes.name: participation_status
attributes.description: Current participation status in the pocket money system

payload.rationale: Child entity represents the business concept of a child participant at the conceptual level, carrying identity and participation attributes necessary for accountability tracking

payload.statement: The TaskCompletion conceptual entity shall realise the relationship: attributes: completion_date date (new), completion_status lifecycle_state (new); relationships to: Child (many-to-one), Task (many-to-one).

attributes.name: completion_date
attributes.description: Date and time the task was completed

payload.rationale: TaskCompletion realises the completion relationship between Child and Task entities, capturing the accountability link between participants and their completed work

payload.statement: The system shall make task-based earnings visible to the child participant.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing the capability to track earnings, enabling the goal of pocket money tracking through task-based compensation visibility

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's task completion.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the completion relationship as an accountability artefact, supporting the child's participation tracking

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion for each child.
payload.rationale: Pocket money tracking process requires the business to maintain records of task completion as a persistent responsibility.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of associated earnings for each child.
payload.rationale: Pocket money tracking process requires the business to maintain records of task-based earnings as a persistent responsibility.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle governing task lifecycle.
payload.rationale: Weekly cycle establishes a temporal governance constraint on task lifecycle transitions, determining when lifecycle boundaries reset.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle governing earnings calculation.
payload.rationale: Weekly cycle establishes a temporal governance constraint on earnings calculation, determining when aggregation boundaries reset.

payload.statement: The business shall enable pocket money tracking through task-based earnings.
payload.rationale: Business goal expressing the fundamental motivation and governance principle for the earnings settlement capability.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks with monetary values.
payload.rationale: How-column Function CCI describing a parent actor interacting with the system (boundary-crossing action). Subject = the actor (parent); verb = their real action (define); object = tasks with monetary values. Actor-subject form required by boundary test.

payload.statement: A child can view available and completed tasks within the weekly timeframe.
payload.rationale: How-column Function CCI describing a child actor interacting with the system (boundary-crossing action). Subject = the actor (child); verb = their real action (view); object = tasks (available and completed) scoped to the weekly timeframe. Actor-subject form required by boundary test.

payload.statement: A task is a business entity with a defined monetary value.
payload.rationale: Recognising task completion as strategic requires defining task as a business entity with monetary value at Row 2.

payload.statement: A child is a business entity participating in the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: Task completion as strategic requires recognising child as the business entity that completes tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a completion relationship linking child entities to task entities.
payload.rationale: Strategic recognition of task completion requires the business to maintain the structural relationship between children and completed tasks.

payload.statement: Weekly earnings is a derived aggregate attribute of the child entity.
payload.rationale: Strategic task completion requires representing aggregated earnings as a structural attribute of participating children.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the parent role as responsible for defining tasks and viewing reports.
payload.rationale: Strategic task completion requires recognising the parent role accountable for task definition and oversight.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the child role as responsible for completing tasks and viewing earnings.
payload.rationale: Strategic task completion requires recognising the child role accountable for task execution and earnings visibility.

payload.statement: The business shall govern task lifecycle and earnings calculation within a weekly business cycle.
payload.rationale: Strategic task completion requires the business to operate task lifecycle and earnings within a defined temporal governance cycle.
